description: In `sign_sync` (lib.rs line 73), the parameter is typed `backend: &Arc<dyn SigningBackend>` instead of `backend: &dyn SigningBackend`. Taking a reference to an `Arc` is a Clippy antipattern (`clippy::clippy::ref_counted_type`): the function only calls `backend.sign(msg)` on it, so it has no need for the `Arc` wrapper. The `&dyn SigningBackend` type would be strictly more general and avoids coupling this private function to the smart pointer type. Callers already hold `Arc`, so the call sites change from `sign_sync(&self.backend, msg)` to the identical expression — `Arc` derefs to the trait object automatically. Fix: change the parameter to `backend: &dyn SigningBackend`.
title: sign_sync: &Arc param should be &dyn
